metadata.release_note: HF card says the final release is step_4000 on main after a 23/02/2026 update.

metadata.lineage_note: Closed/API model; no public training lineage emitted.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu-8B is the RL checkpoint initialized from the released DR-Tulu-SFT-8B checkpoint before online RL training; HF notes the final main release is step_4000 after earlier step_1000 and step_1900 releases.

evidence.location: Model card, DR Tulu-8B section and release note
evidence.excerpt: Our final release is `step_4000` (main and branch).
evidence.explanation: Identifies the subject checkpoint as the final DR Tulu-8B release.

evidence.excerpt: This is the RL checkpoint of DR Tulu, an open deep research agent trained on top of rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B.
evidence.explanation: Directly states that the RL model was trained on top of the SFT checkpoint.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu-8B underwent online RL/GRPO training on the released DR Tulu RL data, whose public HF release contains OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts with search-based rubrics; RaR data used in final RL is represented by separate leaf edges.

evidence.excerpt: This model has undergone RL training on this dataset.
evidence.explanation: The linked dataset is rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data on the model card.

evidence.location: Dataset card, DR Tulu RL Data
evidence.excerpt: This dataset contains the RL training data for DR Tulu, containing prompts and search-based rubrics generated from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts
evidence.explanation: Describes the released RL dataset and its contents.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: OpenScholar prompts are a leaf source for DR Tulu RL prompts: the RL prompt pool collected high-quality long-form prompts from OpenScholar not used during SFT, and the public RL data card says its rubrics are generated from OpenScholar prompts.

evidence.excerpt: we collect approximately 5K new prompts from SearchArena (Miroyan et al., 2025) and OpenScholar (Asai et al., 2024).
evidence.explanation: Names OpenScholar as a source of RL prompts.

evidence.location: Dataset card, DR Tulu RL Data
evidence.excerpt: prompts and search-based rubrics generated from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts
evidence.explanation: Confirms OpenScholar as a released RL-data source.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: SearchArena/Search Arena prompts are a leaf source for DR Tulu RL prompts: the RL prompt pool collected about 5K new prompts from SearchArena and OpenScholar, with search-based rubrics in the released RL data.

evidence.excerpt: we collect approximately 5K new prompts from SearchArena (Miroyan et al., 2025) and OpenScholar (Asai et al., 2024).
evidence.explanation: Names SearchArena as a source of RL prompts.

evidence.location: Dataset card, DR Tulu RL Data
evidence.excerpt: prompts and search-based rubrics generated from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts
evidence.explanation: Confirms SearchArena as a released RL-data source.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The final RL mix sampled 3,000 examples from RaR-Science-20k-o3-mini as additional long-form RL prompt/rubric data beyond the public DR Tulu RL dataset.

evidence.excerpt: we sample 4K prompts from RaR (Gunjal et al., 2025) to enhance further data diversity.
evidence.explanation: The DR Tulu paper states that RaR prompts were used in RL training.

evidence.location: Dataset card, DR Tulu RL Data note
evidence.excerpt: For the RaR data, we use data from: 1. anisha2102/RaR-Science-20k-o3-mini
evidence.explanation: Identifies the exact RaR Science dataset used.

evidence.location: Dataset card, DR Tulu RL Data note
evidence.excerpt: We sample the first 3000 samples from RaR-Science, and the first 1000 samples from RaR-Medicine.
evidence.explanation: Gives the RaR Science sample count used in training.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The final RL mix sampled 1,000 examples from RaR-Medicine-20k-o3-mini as additional long-form RL prompt/rubric data beyond the public DR Tulu RL dataset.

evidence.excerpt: we sample 4K prompts from RaR (Gunjal et al., 2025) to enhance further data diversity.
evidence.explanation: The DR Tulu paper states that RaR prompts were used in RL training.

evidence.location: Dataset card, DR Tulu RL Data note
evidence.excerpt: 2. anisha2102/RaR-Medicine-20k-o3-mini
evidence.explanation: Identifies the exact RaR Medicine dataset used.

evidence.location: Dataset card, DR Tulu RL Data note
evidence.excerpt: We sample the first 3000 samples from RaR-Science, and the first 1000 samples from RaR-Medicine.
evidence.explanation: Gives the RaR Medicine sample count used in training.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 generated initial and evolving rubrics used by RLER during online RL; the released RL data also contains GPT-4.1-generated search-based rubrics for OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts.

evidence.excerpt: we use GPT-4.1 as an rubric generator for both initial and evolving rubrics.
evidence.explanation: Directly documents GPT-4.1 as the rubric generator in the RL training loop.

evidence.location: Dataset card, DR Tulu RL Data
evidence.excerpt: rubrics generated using GPT-4.1.
evidence.explanation: Confirms the released RL data's search-based rubrics were generated with GPT-4.1.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1-mini served as the in-loop LM judge for rubric and citation scoring during RL; its scores formed rewards that selected/weighted rollouts for GRPO updates, so its judgments directly shaped final weights.

evidence.excerpt: For rubric and citation scoring, we use GPT-4.1-mini (gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14) as an LM judge
evidence.explanation: Names GPT-4.1-mini as the judge used to score training rewards.

evidence.excerpt: and obtain two LLM-judge signals: Recall... Precision.
evidence.explanation: Shows that LLM-judge signals were part of the citation reward used during training.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: HealthBench was one of DR Tulu-8B's long-form deep research evaluation benchmarks; the paper evaluates a 1,000-case subset using an adapted OpenAI simple-evals protocol.

evidence.excerpt: We evaluated deep research agents on four long-form, open-ended benchmarks spanning general-domain, scientific, and medical applications: HealthBench
evidence.explanation: Names HealthBench as a main long-form evaluation benchmark.

evidence.excerpt: HealthBench. We use an adapted version of the OpenAI simple-evals suite for the evaluation.
evidence.explanation: Provides evaluation protocol details for HealthBench.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: ResearchQA was used to evaluate DR Tulu-8B's scientific literature synthesis/deep-research performance on the official 776-question subset.

evidence.excerpt: HealthBench, ResearchQA, AstaBench-ScholarQA-CS2 (SQAv2; Asai et al. 2024; Bragg et al. 2025), and DeepResearchBench
evidence.explanation: Names ResearchQA as a main long-form evaluation benchmark.

evidence.excerpt: ResearchQA. We evaluate our method and baselines using the original ResearchQA evaluation suite
evidence.explanation: Confirms ResearchQA was used in the evaluation suite.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: AstaBench-ScholarQA-CS2, reported as SQAv2/ScholarQA-CS2, was a main long-form evaluation benchmark with rubric, answer-precision, and citation metrics.

evidence.excerpt: AstaBench-ScholarQA-CS2 (SQAv2; Asai et al. 2024; Bragg et al. 2025)
evidence.explanation: Names AstaBench-ScholarQA-CS2/SQAv2 as a main evaluation benchmark.

evidence.excerpt: Asta-ScholarQA-CS2. We use the official code to evaluate our method and the baselines on Asta-ScholarQA-CS2.
evidence.explanation: Confirms official Asta-ScholarQA-CS2 evaluation was run.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DeepResearchBench/DRB was used as a long-form general-domain deep research benchmark for DR Tulu-8B, including English and Chinese questions and article-quality metrics.

evidence.excerpt: DeepResearchBench (DRB; Du et al. 2025).
evidence.explanation: Names DeepResearchBench as a main long-form evaluation benchmark.

evidence.excerpt: DeepResearchBench. We use the official code to evaluate our method and the baselines on DeepResearchBench.
evidence.explanation: Confirms DRB official evaluation was used.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GeneticDiseasesQA was a newly constructed out-of-domain medical-genetics deep research evaluation set of 47 questions derived from expert-curated information about 24 pathogenic variants.

evidence.excerpt: GeneticDiseasesQA. We create a new evaluation dataset, GeneticDiseasesQA, consisting of 47 questions
evidence.explanation: Names and describes the GeneticDiseasesQA evaluation dataset.

evidence.excerpt: We manually reformatted this data into 47 question-answer examples.
evidence.explanation: Gives construction details for the evaluation dataset.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: SimpleQA was part of the short-form QA evaluation suite used to analyze how DR Tulu's SFT and RL stages generalized beyond long-form deep research.

evidence.excerpt: Our short-form evaluation suite comprises SimpleQA (Wei et al., 2024), WebWalkerQA (Wu et al., 2025a), and 2Wiki
evidence.explanation: Names SimpleQA as a short-form evaluation benchmark.

evidence.excerpt: Table 5 Short-form results. We report short-form performance for our SFT and RL variants
evidence.explanation: Shows the benchmark was used to report DR Tulu results.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: 2Wiki was part of the short-form QA evaluation suite; the authors randomly selected 1,000 2Wiki questions for efficient evaluation.

evidence.excerpt: Our short-form evaluation suite comprises SimpleQA (Wei et al., 2024), WebWalkerQA (Wu et al., 2025a), and 2Wiki
evidence.explanation: Names 2Wiki as a short-form evaluation benchmark.

evidence.excerpt: We randomly selected 1000 questions each from SimpleQA and 2Wiki for efficient evaluation.
evidence.explanation: Provides the 2Wiki evaluation sample size.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: WebWalkerQA/WebWalker was part of DR Tulu-8B's short-form QA evaluation suite reported alongside SimpleQA and 2Wiki.

evidence.excerpt: Our short-form evaluation suite comprises SimpleQA (Wei et al., 2024), WebWalkerQA (Wu et al., 2025a), and 2Wiki
evidence.explanation: Names WebWalkerQA as a short-form evaluation benchmark.

evidence.excerpt: Benchmark SQAv2 HealthBench ResearchQA DeepResearch Bench SimpleQA 2Wiki WebWalker Average
evidence.explanation: The model card reports WebWalker results for DR Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 was used as an LLM judge for DR Tulu evaluations: GeneticDiseasesQA category scoring, the unified short-form QA pipeline, and the paper notes HealthBench also used GPT-4.1.

evidence.excerpt: using GPT-4.1 as a judge to score each category
evidence.explanation: Documents GPT-4.1 as the GeneticDiseasesQA judge.

evidence.excerpt: All scores are computed from top-1 predictions and evaluated with GPT-4.1 as the LLM judge
evidence.explanation: Documents GPT-4.1 as the short-form QA evaluation judge.

evidence.excerpt: Healthbench uses GPT-4.1, all with varying prompts and evaluation harnesses
evidence.explanation: Documents GPT-4.1 as the HealthBench downstream evaluation judge.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1-mini was used as the ResearchQA evaluation judge to compute averaged rubric scores on the 776-question official subset.

evidence.excerpt: compute the averaged rubric scores with GPT-4.1-mini as the judge on the 776 official subset of questions
evidence.explanation: Directly names GPT-4.1-mini as the ResearchQA evaluation judge and gives the subset size.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: Gemini 2.5 Flash was used as an LLM judge in downstream long-form evaluations: Asta-ScholarQA-CS2 and DeepResearchBench scoring.

evidence.excerpt: using gemini-2.5-flash as the judge as detailed in Bragg et al. (2025).
evidence.explanation: Documents Gemini 2.5 Flash as the Asta-ScholarQA-CS2 judge.

evidence.excerpt: We use gemini-2.5-flash as the judge
evidence.explanation: Documents Gemini 2.5 Flash as the DeepResearchBench judge.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-No-RLER-8B is an officially released ablation checkpoint trained without RLER to analyze the effect of the evolving-rubric method relative to the main DR Tulu-8B model.

evidence.location: Model card, top note and DR Tulu-No-RLER-8B section
evidence.excerpt: This is an ablation model! Checkpoints are saved as branches on this repo. Our real model is `rl-research/DR-Tulu-8B`.
evidence.explanation: Identifies No-RLER as an ablation model for DR Tulu.

evidence.excerpt: This model is trained without RLER, and is an ablation model for analysing the effect of RLER.
evidence.explanation: States the ablated component and purpose.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The team ablated the SFT training-data mixture and scale: 0%, 5%, 10%, 100%, long-form-only, and short-form-only variants were run to understand how SFT data affected downstream RL and evaluation performance.

evidence.excerpt: We ablate SFT training data in terms of the mixture of data and scale of training data.
evidence.explanation: States that the SFT data mix/scale was ablated.

evidence.excerpt: we used the full-size SFT dataset to maintain strong short-form QA abilities
evidence.explanation: Explains why the full SFT dataset was chosen after ablation.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: SQAv2/Asta-ScholarQA-CS2 scores were used in DR Tulu ablations of SFT-data scale/mixture, RL starting checkpoints, and rubric variants.

evidence.excerpt: Figure 6 Ablation of SFT training data... SQAv2
evidence.explanation: Shows SQAv2 was a metric in the SFT-data ablation.

evidence.excerpt: Table 6 Ablation on rubrics. We ablate the rubrics used during RL training
evidence.explanation: Table 6 includes SQAv2 as an ablation metric for rubric variants.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: HealthBench scores were used in DR Tulu's SFT-data, RL starting-point, and rubric ablations to guide training decisions.

evidence.excerpt: Figure 7 Performance of different starting point models throughout RL training.
evidence.explanation: The plotted ablation panels include HealthBench scores for different RL starting models.

evidence.excerpt: Table 6 Ablation on rubrics. We ablate the rubrics used during RL training
evidence.explanation: Table 6 uses HealthBench as an ablation metric for rubric choices.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: ResearchQA scores were used in DR Tulu ablations of SFT-data scale/mixture and rubric variants.

evidence.location: Figure 6 caption and plot labels, page 14
evidence.excerpt: Figure 6 Ablation of SFT training data.
evidence.explanation: The Figure 6 ablation panels include ResearchQA.

evidence.excerpt: SQAv2 HealthBench ResearchQA DRB Avg.
evidence.explanation: ResearchQA appears as a metric in the rubric ablation table.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DeepResearchBench/DRB scores were used in SFT-data, RL starting-point, and rubric ablations for DR Tulu.

evidence.excerpt: DRB
evidence.explanation: DRB is one of the plotted metrics in the training ablations.

evidence.excerpt: SQAv2 HealthBench ResearchQA DRB Avg.
evidence.explanation: DRB appears as a metric in the rubric ablation table.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: 2Wiki scores were used in the SFT-data ablation to show that removing short-form data noticeably reduced short-form performance.

evidence.excerpt: 2Wiki
evidence.explanation: The SFT-data ablation figure includes a 2Wiki panel.

evidence.excerpt: removing short-form data leaves HealthBench, ResearchQA, and DRB largely unchanged, but noticeably reduces performance on 2Wiki and SQAv2.
evidence.explanation: Explains the ablation conclusion using 2Wiki.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: The DR Tulu SFT checkpoint was supervised-finetuned from Qwen3-8B, not from Qwen3-8B-Base directly.

evidence.location: Model card, DR Tulu SFT 8B section
evidence.excerpt: This is the SFT checkpoint of DR Tulu, an open deep research agent trained on top of Qwen3-8B.
evidence.explanation: Directly states the SFT model's base checkpoint.

evidence.excerpt: Base model Qwen/Qwen3-8B-Base Finetuned Qwen/Qwen3-8B Finetuned this model
evidence.explanation: Shows the HF model-tree chain through Qwen3-8B into DR-Tulu-SFT-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B was trained on the DR Tulu SFT data release, containing prompts and full teacher trajectories with reasoning traces, tool calls, and citation-backed answers.

evidence.location: Model card, DR Tulu SFT 8B section
evidence.excerpt: This model has undergone SFT training on this dataset.
evidence.explanation: The linked dataset is rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-data.

evidence.location: Dataset card, DR Tulu SFT Data
evidence.excerpt: containing prompts and full trajectories including reasoning traces, tool calls, and answers with citations.
evidence.explanation: Describes the contents of the SFT training data.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: OpenScholar was a long-form prompt source for SFT: the paper says long-form prompts came from OpenScholar and SearchArena, and Table 8 lists 5,704 OpenScholar SFT instances.

evidence.excerpt: we derive prompts from publicly available user-assistant interaction data: SearchArena... and OpenScholar

evidence.excerpt: OpenScholar Long-form 5704 3.5 3878.7

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: SearchArena/Search Arena was a long-form prompt source for SFT; Table 8 lists 3,547 Search Arena SFT instances after curation and trajectory generation.

evidence.explanation: Names SearchArena as an SFT prompt source.

evidence.excerpt: Search Arena Long-form 3547 3.1 2745.9

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: ScholarQA/Ai2 ScholarQA traces were transformed into 1,000 long-form iterative-search SFT examples by interleaving sub-queries, retrieved papers, CoT planning, and final answers.

evidence.excerpt: ScholarQA Long-form 1000 5.4 5400.5
evidence.explanation: Lists ScholarQA as a 1,000-instance SFT source.

evidence.excerpt: We used the trajectory data from Ai2 ScholarQA to create SFT data.
evidence.explanation: Explains how ScholarQA traces entered SFT data.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: HotpotQA was one of the short-form verifiable QA sources mixed into SFT so DR Tulu learned to handle concise answer styles; Table 8 lists 1,176 HotpotQA SFT instances.

evidence.excerpt: we sample from HotpotQA... TaskCraft... WebWalker-Silver... and MegaScience
evidence.explanation: Names MegaScience as a short-form SFT source.

evidence.excerpt: MegaScience Short-form 814 2.3 1494.8

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B trained on 916 synthetic BrowseComp-style short-form prompts generated for the project, not necessarily the official BrowseComp benchmark itself.

evidence.excerpt: We also used GPT-4.1 to generate 916 BrowseComp (Wei et al., 2025) style questions.
evidence.explanation: Documents the synthetic BrowseComp-style prompt source and count.

evidence.excerpt: BrowseComp Short-form 916 8.6 4083.5

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: PopQA-inspired/generated short-form questions were included in the SFT mix together with TyDi QA; Table 8 groups PopQA & TyDi QA into 874 SFT instances.

evidence.excerpt: we generate additional challenging synthetic prompts inspired by PopQA (Mallen et al., 2023).
evidence.explanation: States that PopQA inspired additional synthetic SFT prompts.

evidence.excerpt: PopQA & TyDi QA Short-form 874 3.7 1514.3
evidence.explanation: Lists the combined PopQA/TyDi QA SFT row.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: TyDi QA English was included in the short-form SFT mix together with PopQA; Table 8 groups PopQA & TyDi QA into 874 SFT instances.

evidence.excerpt: including MegaScience... HotpotQA... TaskCraft... WebWalkerSilver... PopQA... and TyDi QA
evidence.explanation: Names TyDi QA among initial short-form question sources.

evidence.excerpt: PopQA & TyDi QA Short-form 874 3.7 1514.3
evidence.explanation: Lists the combined PopQA/TyDi QA SFT row.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: GPT-5 generated SFT teacher trajectories, including explicit mock thinking traces, tool calls, and final citation-backed answers, using the DR Tulu search inference pipeline.

evidence.excerpt: We provide GPT-5 with a detailed system prompt... and ask it to produce the entire trajectory.
evidence.explanation: States that GPT-5 generated full SFT trajectories.

evidence.excerpt: We generated trajectories using GPT-5 and our search inference pipeline
evidence.explanation: Confirms GPT-5 trajectory generation for SFT data.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: GPT-5 acted as the long-form prompt-quality judge for SFT prompt curation, scoring prompts 1-5 and retaining only prompts above source-specific thresholds.

evidence.excerpt: An LM (gpt-5) scores each prompt from 1–5 (higher is better)
evidence.explanation: Names GPT-5 as the SFT prompt-quality scoring model.

evidence.excerpt: we retain prompts with scores> 3 for OpenScholar and prompts with scores >2 for SearchArena.
evidence.explanation: Shows GPT-5 scores were used to filter SFT prompts.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 generated 916 BrowseComp-style SFT questions and generated section-level sub-queries used to convert Ai2 ScholarQA traces into iterative-search SFT data.

evidence.excerpt: We also used GPT-4.1 to generate 916 BrowseComp (Wei et al., 2025) style questions.
evidence.explanation: Documents GPT-4.1 generation of a named SFT subset.

evidence.excerpt: We used GPT-4.1 to create a sub-query for each section in the Ai2 ScholarQA.
evidence.explanation: Documents GPT-4.1-generated sub-queries in the ScholarQA SFT conversion.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-sft-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 transformed Ai2 ScholarQA traces into iterative-search SFT examples by generating sub-queries conditioned on each section and cited papers, then interleaving sub-queries, retrieved papers, CoT plan reasoning, and the final answer.

evidence.excerpt: To create synthetic data with iterative searches, we transformed the data provided in the Ai2 ScholarQA traces.
evidence.explanation: States that Ai2 ScholarQA traces were transformed for SFT.

evidence.excerpt: The sub-query was generated conditioned on the section text and retrieved papers cited in the section.
evidence.explanation: Explains GPT-4.1's transformation role in creating iterative-search traces.

edges.subject: qwen/qwen3-8b

edges.subject: qwen/qwen3-8b-base
edges.description: Qwen3-8B-Base was pretrained on Qwen3's internal 36T-token corpus spanning 119 languages and mixing coding, STEM, reasoning, books, multilingual, and synthetic data; the exact leaf corpora are not publicly named.

evidence.explanation: Documents the size and language coverage of the Qwen3 pretraining corpus.

evidence.excerpt: including coding, STEM, reasoning, book, multilingual, and synthetic data.
evidence.explanation: Describes the named categories in the Qwen3 pretraining corpus.

edges.description: The No-RLER ablation checkpoint underwent RL training on the DR Tulu RL dataset while disabling the RLER mechanism.
